My Personal Trader
Runs on your own MT5 account

Algorithmic trading, sized for how you actually trade

My Personal Trader is a rules-based execution bot for MT5. Connect your own broker account, pick a risk profile that fits your capital — from $100 upward — and let the system manage entries, exits, and position sizing.

Trading forex on margin carries a high level of risk and may not be suitable for all investors. Read the full risk disclosure.

Sample trades logged

Plans sized to your capital

Every tier runs the same core engine. What changes is position sizing and risk ceiling, matched to your account size.

Lot sizes and risk parameters are indicative starting points, automatically adjusted by the bot's position-sizing engine as your account balance changes. All trading involves risk of loss.

From signup to live trading in six steps

The bot is a standalone desktop app that connects to your already-running MT5 terminal — it never modifies MT5 itself. Your funds stay with your broker — we never take custody.

Want to try it risk-free first?

You can open a free demo account with your broker of choice and connect the app to it — test any of the models on simulated funds for as long as you'd like before ever trading with real money.

Supported brokers

Any MT5 broker that allows algorithmic/EA trading and hedging. Full compatibility list sent after signup.

On a Mac?

Just make sure MT5 for Mac is installed and you're logged into your account before opening the app — everything else needed to connect runs automatically the first time you launch it.

Seeing a security warning when you first open the app?

Since we're still waiting on official approval from Microsoft and Apple for our software, you may see a security warning the first time you open the app. This is expected and safe to proceed past. On Windows, click "More info" then "Run anyway." On Mac, right-click (or Control-click) the app icon, choose "Open," then confirm — you'll only need to do this once. If you run into any issues, please and we'll help you get set up.

!

Risk warning

Trading foreign exchange on margin carries a high level of risk and may not be suitable for all investors. Leverage can work against you as well as for you. Before deciding to trade, you should carefully consider your investment objectives, level of experience, and risk appetite. You could sustain a loss of some or all of your initial investment. Do not invest money you cannot afford to lose.
